paper_title: Study on PFAS in Korean Patients with Pollinosis

methods.description: The process of designing the study and selecting participants for a nationwide, cross-sectional study on PFAS conducted in South Korea.

steps.description: Data were collected from patients diagnosed with pollinosis at 21 institutes (19 university hospitals and two allergy clinics).
steps.searchable_summary: collect patient data from 21 medical institutes

steps.description: The protocol was reviewed and approved by the institutional review boards of each institute.
steps.searchable_summary: obtain ethical approval from institutional review boards

steps.description: Written informed consent was obtained from each patient or their parents.
Pollinosis was diagnosed according to criteria including allergic diseases, sensitization to pollen, and aggravated symptoms.
steps.searchable_summary: diagnose pollinosis based on specific criteria

steps.description: Sensitization to pollen was diagnosed by positive results to allergy skin tests and/or high serum-specific IgE levels.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze allergy test results for pollen sensitization

methods.description: Analysis of data collected from patients with PFAS, including demographics, list of culprit fruits and vegetables, and clinical manifestations of food allergy.

steps.description: Data from 273 patients with PFAS were collected, including demographics, list of culprit fruits and vegetables, and clinical manifestations of food allergy.
steps.searchable_summary: collect PFAS patient data including demographics and allergy details

steps.description: We analyzed 27 anaphylaxis patients and compared them with patients with PFAS with oropharyngeal symptoms only (n=130).
steps.searchable_summary: compare anaphylaxis patients with PFAS patients with oropharyngeal symptoms
